div.h2: EPOLLOUT Busy Loop Anti-Pattern vs Correct Lifecycle

g.text: CORRECT — Minimal epoll_ctl Calls, Zero Busy Loop

div.p: The EPOLLOUT Invariant (must hold at all times): epollout_armed == 1 ↔ write_buf_pending(conn->write_buf) > 0 ↔ EPOLLOUT ∈ epoll interest set All three conditions are simultaneously true or simultaneously false. Any violation → busy loop or data loss. Cost breakdown per backpressure event: 2× epoll_ctl(MOD) syscalls (~400ns each) = ~800ns total overhead. Cost of missing disarm: epoll_wait returns at 10M/sec × 0 useful work = one full CPU core wasted permanently.
❌ EPOLLOUT registered at connection creation ev.events = EPOLLIN | EPOLLOUT — NEVER do this
✅ EPOLLOUT disarmed at creation — armed only when write buffer has data ev.events = EPOLLIN — EPOLLOUT added only on EAGAIN

p.code: epoll_ctl(MOD) calls: exactly 2 per backpressure event
